or **Sundays after Trinity**, is a [liturgical period](/wiki/Liturgical_year "Liturgical year"), celebrated by some [Christian](/wiki/Christianity "Christianity") churches, which immediately follows the [Easter season](/wiki/Eastertide "Eastertide"). Although the start and end dates vary by liturgical tradition, the season typically begins on the day of [Pentecost](/wiki/Pentecost "Pentecost") and continues throughout the rest of the [liturgical year](/wiki/Liturgical_year "Liturgical year"), ending just before the season of [Advent](/wiki/Advent "Advent"). The [liturgical color](/wiki/Liturgical_color "Liturgical color") for this period is typically green or red.

[*The Book of Common Prayer*](https://episcopalchurch.org/files/book_of_common_prayer.pdf) (PDF). [The Episcopal Church](/wiki/Episcopal_Church_(United_States) "Episcopal Church (United States)"). p. 32.

first_paragraph: **Pentecost season**, also known **Pentecostide**, as well as the time of **Sundays after Pentecost** or **Sundays after Trinity**, is a [liturgical period](/wiki/Liturgical_year "Liturgical year"), celebrated by some [Christian](/wiki/Christianity "Christianity") churches, which immediately follows the [Easter season](/wiki/Eastertide "Eastertide"). Although the start and end dates vary by liturgical tradition, the season typically begins on the day of [Pentecost](/wiki/Pentecost "Pentecost") and continues throughout the rest of the [liturgical year](/wiki/Liturgical_year "Liturgical year"), ending just before the season of [Advent](/wiki/Advent "Advent"). The [liturgical color](/wiki/Liturgical_color "Liturgical color") for this period is typically green or red.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Liturgical season

| [Liturgical seasons](/wiki/Liturgical_year "Liturgical year") |
| --- |
| * Pre-Christmas   + [Advent](/wiki/Advent "Advent") (*Western*)   + [Nativity Fast](/wiki/Nativity_Fast "Nativity Fast") (*Byzantine*)   + [Annunciation](/wiki/Season_of_Annunciation "Season of Annunciation") (*Syriac*) * [Christmas](/wiki/Christmastide "Christmastide") * [Epiphany](/wiki/Epiphany_season "Epiphany season")   + [Ordinary Time](/wiki/Ordinary_Time "Ordinary Time") (*Western*) * [Pre-Lent](/wiki/Pre-Lent "Pre-Lent") * [Lent](/wiki/Lent "Lent") (*Western*) / [Great Lent](/wiki/Great_Lent "Great Lent") (*Eastern*) * [Paschal Triduum](/wiki/Paschal_Triduum "Paschal Triduum") (*Western*) * [Easter](/wiki/Eastertide "Eastertide") * Pentecost   + [Ordinary Time](/wiki/Ordinary_Time "Ordinary Time") (*Western*) * [Summer](/wiki/Season_of_Summer "Season of Summer") (*East Syriac*) * [Apostles' Fast](/wiki/Apostles%27_Fast "Apostles' Fast") (*Eastern*) * [Dormition Fast](/wiki/Dormition_Fast "Dormition Fast") (*Eastern*) * [Elijah–Cross–Moses](/w/index.php?title=Elijah%E2%80%93Cross%E2%80%93Moses&action=edit&redlink=1 "Elijah–Cross–Moses (page does not exist)") (*East Syriac*) * [Dedication of the Church](/w/index.php?title=Season_of_the_Dedication_of_the_Church&action=edit&redlink=1 "Season of the Dedication of the Church (page does not exist)") (*Syriac*) |
| * [v](/wiki/Template:Liturgical_year "Template:Liturgical year") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Liturgical_year "Template talk:Liturgical year") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Liturgical_year "Special:EditPage/Template:Liturgical year") |

**Pentecost season**, also known **Pentecostide**, as well as the time of **Sundays after Pentecost** or **Sundays after Trinity**, is a [liturgical period](/wiki/Liturgical_year "Liturgical year"), celebrated by some [Christian](/wiki/Christianity "Christianity") churches, which immediately follows the [Easter season](/wiki/Eastertide "Eastertide"). Although the start and end dates vary by liturgical tradition, the season typically begins on the day of [Pentecost](/wiki/Pentecost "Pentecost") and continues throughout the rest of the [liturgical year](/wiki/Liturgical_year "Liturgical year"), ending just before the season of [Advent](/wiki/Advent "Advent"). The [liturgical color](/wiki/Liturgical_color "Liturgical color") for this period is typically green or red.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ec/Lutherkirche_Altar.jpg/250px-Lutherkirche_Altar.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lutherkirche_Altar.jpg)

Red is the liturgical colour assigned to Pentecost Sunday, the first day of the Pentecost season in Western Christianity; depicted is the [chancel](/wiki/Chancel "Chancel") of Lutherkirche in the German city of [Schöneberg](/wiki/Sch%C3%B6neberg "Schöneberg").

## Western Christianity

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pentecost_season&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Western Christianity")]

### Roman Rite

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pentecost_season&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Roman Rite")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **does not [cite](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this section](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Pentecost_season "Special:EditPage/Pentecost season") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *(April 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

The [Ordinary Form](/wiki/Ordinary_Form "Ordinary Form") of the [Roman Rite](/wiki/Roman_Rite "Roman Rite") of the [Catholic Church](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") does not include a Pentecost season. Pentecost is considered the last day of the [Easter season](/wiki/Eastertide "Eastertide"), and is followed by [Ordinary Time](/wiki/Ordinary_Time "Ordinary Time").

[Traditionalist Catholicism](/wiki/Traditionalist_Catholicism "Traditionalist Catholicism") has an eight-day [Octave](/wiki/Octave_(liturgy) "Octave (liturgy)") of Pentecost, followed by Sundays after Pentecost that continue through to the end of the liturgical year.

The Catholic Church's [Ordinariate Use for former Anglicans](/wiki/Anglican_Use "Anglican Use") uses [Trinity Sunday](/wiki/Trinity_Sunday "Trinity Sunday") (the [octave day](/wiki/Octave_(liturgy) "Octave (liturgy)") of Pentecost) as the start of a liturgical period, with subsequent Sundays designated "Sundays after Trinity".

The various forms of the Roman Rite all use the color green for this period.

### Moravianism

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pentecost_season&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Moravianism")]

In the [Moravian Church](/wiki/Moravian_Church "Moravian Church"), the Pentecost season runs from the Feast of Pentecost itself to the [Reign of Christ](/wiki/Feast_of_Christ_the_King "Feast of Christ the King"), the last Sunday of the liturgical year.[[1]](#cite_note-Moravian2012-1) Red is the liturgical color used for Pentecost Sunday; white is the liturgical color used for Trinity Sunday and Reign of Christ Sunday; green is the liturgical color used for the other Sundays of the Pentecost season.[[1]](#cite_note-Moravian2012-1)

### Lutheranism

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pentecost_season&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Lutheranism")]

In the [Lutheran Church–Missouri Synod](/wiki/Lutheran_Church%E2%80%93Missouri_Synod "Lutheran Church–Missouri Synod"), this period can be called "Season after Pentecost" or "Season of the Holy Trinity."[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### Anglicanism

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pentecost_season&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Anglicanism")]

In classical prayer books around the world, it is referred to as Trinitytide, "Season of Trinity", or just "Sundays after Trinity"

In [The Episcopal Church](/wiki/The_Episcopal_Church "The Episcopal Church") the *[Book of Common Prayer](/wiki/Book_of_Common_Prayer_(1979) "Book of Common Prayer (1979)")* refers to this period as the "Season after Pentecost."[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

### Reformed

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pentecost_season&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Reformed")]

In the [Reformed Churches](/wiki/Reformed_Church "Reformed Church"), such as the [Presbyterian Church (USA)](/wiki/Presbyterian_Church_(USA) "Presbyterian Church (USA)"), the season is known as the "Time after Pentecost".[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The [United Church of Christ](/wiki/United_Church_of_Christ "United Church of Christ"), a [Congregationalist](/wiki/Congregationalist "Congregationalist") denomination in the Reformed tradition, assigns the period of the liturgical calendar as "Time after Pentecost".[[6]](#cite_note-6)

### Methodism

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pentecost_season&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Methodism")]

Many Annual Conferences within the [United Methodist Church](/wiki/United_Methodist_Church "United Methodist Church") observe [Kingdomtide](/wiki/Kingdomtide "Kingdomtide") as a liturgical season spanning the latter portion of the period between [Pentecost](/wiki/Pentecost "Pentecost") and the start of the [Advent](/wiki/Advent "Advent") season.[[7]](#cite_note-FCOO2013-7)

## Eastern Christianity

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pentecost_season&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Eastern Christianity")]

### Byzantine Rite

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pentecost_season&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Byzantine Rite")]

The [Byzantine Rite](/wiki/Byzantine_Rite "Byzantine Rite") of [Eastern Christianity](/wiki/Eastern_Christianity "Eastern Christianity") uses the nomenclature of "Sundays after Pentecost."[[8]](#cite_note-8)

### West Syriac Rite

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pentecost_season&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: West Syriac Rite")]

The [Maronite Church](/wiki/Maronite_Church "Maronite Church"), which uses the [West Syriac Rite](/wiki/West_Syriac_Rite "West Syriac Rite"), celebrates a Pentecost season that extends until the [Feast of the Exaltation of the Cross](/wiki/Feast_of_the_Cross "Feast of the Cross").

### East Syriac Rite

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pentecost_season&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: East Syriac Rite")]

Main article: [Season of Apostles](/wiki/Season_of_Apostles "Season of Apostles")

The closest equivalent to a Pentecost season in the [East Syriac Rite](/wiki/East_Syriac_Rite "East Syriac Rite") is the [Season of Apostles](/wiki/Season_of_Apostles "Season of Apostles"), which begins with [Pentecost](/wiki/Pentecost "Pentecost") and continues for seven weeks.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
